title: serratula cichoracea subsp. mucronata
intro: Serratula cichoracea subsp. mucronata, also known as the prickly knapweed or prickly serratula, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Serratula cichoracea subsp. mucronata include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. It can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, with the dried leaves and stems being commonly used for medicinal purposes.
